Class GameChoices

java.lang.Object
fr.uge.cascadia.game.GameChoices
public class GameChoices extends Object
Provides utility methods for gathering player input and game settings.

The GameChoices class handles user interaction to configure the game, including selecting the game variant, mode, number of players, and player names.

  • Constructor Details

    • GameChoices

      public GameChoices()

  • Method Details

    • variantChoice

      public static int variantChoice()
      Prompts the user to choose a game variant.

      The available variants are:

      • 1 - Family mode
      • 2 - Intermediate mode
      • 3 - Animal cards mode
      Returns:
      the selected variant as an integer (1, 2, or 3)

    • cardChoice

      public static String cardChoice()
      Prompts the user to choose a card for the "Animal cards" game mode.

      The available cards are:

      • A
      • B
      • C
      • D
      Returns:
      the selected card as an uppercase string ("A", "B", "C", or "D")

    • gameModeChoice

      public static int gameModeChoice()
      Prompts the user to choose a game mode.

      The available modes are:

      • 1 - Play in the terminal
      • 2 - Play with a graphical interface (square tiles)
      • 3 - Play with a graphical interface (hexagonal tiles)
      Returns:
      the selected mode as an integer (1, 2, or 3)

    • modeChoice

      public static int modeChoice()

    • numberPlayersChoice

      public static int numberPlayersChoice()
      Prompts the user to choose the number of players for the game.

      The allowed range for the number of players is between 1 and 4 inclusive.

      Returns:
      the number of players as an integer (1, 2, 3, or 4)

    • playerNames

      public static List<String> playerNames(int nbPlayers)
      Prompts the user to enter the names of all players.

      The number of names requested corresponds to the number of players selected. Each name must be non-empty, and duplicate names are allowed.

      Parameters:
      nbPlayers - the number of players
      Returns:
      a List of player names as strings
